observations.recommendation: We recommended: a. The Inventory Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, prepare and maintain an Inventory of all Local Roads and a Road Map. Likewise, the preparation of Report on RLRN and RPCLRN must be observed yearly; and b. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, identify the components of each completed projects and compute the remaining road assets as per component accordingly. Moreover, make the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize any differences in reconciliation of accounting records and the required inventory reports.
observations.managementAction: There is already an ongoing inventory of all Local Roads and Road Map. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, prepare and identify the components of each completed projects and compute the remaining road assets as per component accordingly. Moreover, make the necessary adjusting journal entries to recognize any differences in reconciliation of accounting records and the required inventory reports.
observations.reason: Inventory of Road Networks does not show a break down by component and report.

observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to record the land and
process the transfer and titling of the land.

2.6 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accounting
Section evaluate and strengthen internal controls related to cash
handling and recording processes to prevent similar errors in the future.

3.6 We recommended and Management agreed for the Accountant to:
a. Make a reversing/adjusting entry to restate the PPEs derecognized
without specific authority from COA in compliance with Paragraph
7.11 (d) of COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Follow the step-by-step procedural guidelines stipulated in same COA
Circular mentioned above in availing of the One-time Cleansing of
non-existing/missing PPEs of the LGU.

4.4 We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to make the
necessary adjustments on the affected accounts in the financial
statements.

5.6 We recommended and the Accounting Section agreed to:
a. Make the necessary correcting entries to bring the affected accounts
to their correct balances; and
b. Provide disclosure in the notes to financial statements regarding the
capitalization of the land account below the capitalization threshold.

6.7 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the
guideline in granting cash advance for Confidential Fund. Cash advances
shall be limited to amount allowed in COA DBM Joint Circular No. 2015-01
dated January 08, 2015 and in accordance to the approved Physical and
Financial Plan. Further, the DV shall clearly state the duration of the
projects and must be submitted within the time specified in the
abovementioned circular.

7.8 We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the Sections 3.4
and 3.7 of GPPB Resolution No. 018-2006 dated December 6, 2006.

8.5 We recommended and Management agreed to include the material test
results as part of documentary requirements provided to the Office of the
Auditor in the submission of request for inspection in order to ensure the
quality of the materials used for infrastructure projects.

9.7 We recommended that Management collect from the contractor the
deficiency amounting to ₱412,077.30, otherwise the same will be
disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed for the Accounting and GSO Units to reconcile their reports in order to establish the validity and correctness of the Inventory and the corresponding Supplies and Materials Expense account is understated in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: List of recommended beneficiaries were already secured to close out the inventory since the variance was due to the pendling list of recipients for the assets in distribution and were not recorded by the GSO. The accounting department prepare the necessary journal entry and submitted the same to COA during the monthly submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed for the Local Finance Committee and other officials concerned to make use of the cash flow analysis in entering into contracts and to ensure that cash is available and sufficient before entering into contracts and certify availability of funds. Prioritize claims, enhance collection of revenues and undertake strategies to enhance COA result and shown in the notes to FS.
observations.managementAction: The cause for this as explained in the letter reply was the spent portion of the Trust Fund which were still recognized in the liabilities section since the counterpart monitoring cash inflows and cash outflows, ensure that cash is available and sufficient before entering into contracts and certifying availability of funds. Prioritize claims, enhance collection of revenues and undertake strategies to totally eliminate the cash deficit. The completion of the project actually already moved to close and thus were also closed. No overdraft was made as opposed to what was initially shown in the COA result and were already explained initially and fully substantiated in the notes to FS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to increase the amount of bond of the Revenue Collection Clerks and Cashier to adequately cover their cash accountabilities and allow indemnity in case of theft or loss by force majeure.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers have increased the amount of the Revenue Collection Clerks and Cashier to adequately cover their cash accountabilities and allow indemnity in case of theft or loss by force majeure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to: a. Apply for the write-off of the dormant accounts which have been long outstanding in accordance with the guidelines in COA Circular 2016-005 and; b. Strictly comply with the rules and regulations provided in the PD 1445 and the abovementioned COA Circulars.
observations.managementAction: The outstanding CIF is already subject to closure upon completion of the process and release of the clearance to the claimant in accordance with the guidelines in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to direct responsible persons to carry out the procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE balances in order to establish the validity and correctness of the PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: One-Time Cleansing is completed pending Sale of Unserviceable PPEs and Deed of Donation to DepEd.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to provide the results of the material testing as well as the result of the testing to ensure the quality of the materials to be used for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and such Management and the results of the testing is hereby required. Material Testing billing is required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply to Section 1.1 of Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the issuance of variation order.
observations.managementAction: The Management explained that VO was necessary to obtain the desired pump power and capacity as the original pump design has insufficient power. However, the price difference resulting to non-compliance with the 10% threshold. They take full responsibility for this oversight and will take measures to prevent it from happening again.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to fast-track the implementation of the abovementioned project to maximize efficiency of government expenditures and to minimize the risk in the communities in case of calamities and disasters.
observations.managementAction: On-going Construction Management agreed to fast-track the implementation of the abovementioned project to maximize efficiency of government expenditures and to minimize the risk in the communities in case of calamities and disasters.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to include the material test results as part of documentary requirements provided to the Office of the Auditor in the submission of request for inspection in order to ensure the quality of the materials used for infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed and such recommendation is well taken and hereby provide the mechanism and results of the testing as well as the result of the testing to ensure the quality of the materials to be used for infrastructure projects. Material testing results for listed projects were submitted.

observations.recommendation: 1.6 We recommended the Accounting Office to observe paragraph 14 of IPSAS 17 in recognizing the cost of an item of property, plant, and equipment as an asset and classifying to capital expenditures in accordance with item 2.3 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, and make the necessary adjustments to recognize the appropriate asset accounts.
2.7 We recommended the Accounting Office to observe paragraph 82 of IPSAS 17 in derecognizing an item of property, plant, and equipment and properly derecognize the distributed equipment.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request for copies of the bank credit memoranda to facilitate the required adjustments in the books. Further, ensure that long outstanding checks are reverted to Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account to accurately state the cash in bank balance as of the end of the year.
4.7 We recommended that the Management stop the use of the Petty Cash Fund to pay for these recurring expenses pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

5.5 We recommended that the BAC Chairperson meticulously and thoroughly evaluate the proposals submitted by the participating bidders to ensure that the contract be awarded to the qualified bidder and adhere to the guidelines stipulated in the IRR of RA 9184 for the procurement of infrastructure projects.

6.9 We recommended:
a. The technical personnel who prepared the SWA, Project Inspection Report and other related reports to be prudent in doing their responsibilities;
b. To require the contractor of the project to immediately rectify the whole stretch of road shoulder considering that it is still within the warranty period under Section 62 of IRR of RA 9184. Also, oversized granular materials should be removed as it does not conform with the requirement for Item 200; and
c. The concerned officials to submit the Quality Control Program (QCP), not only in this contract, but also in the next infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management exert maximum effort to collect the outstanding receivables from identified beneficiaries and employees through issuance of demand letters and continually follow-up to enforce collection.
observations.managementAction: Issuance of Notices of Collection are still ongoing.

Established the list of inventories based on the book value in the submitted report of the GSO to the Accounting office. Instructed the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all Inventory and PPE accounts and submit the Report of Physical Count not later than January 31 each year. Prepared necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items. Instructed the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its effort to locate pertinent source documents.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of book records is ongoing. Not yet part of the work plan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Ensure that the monthly utilization report and status of implementation of PPAs under the BGCM fund is posted in its website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality; and

b. Send written notice that the said reports were posted on its website to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, and other offices where the submission of reports is required in compliance with Item No. 4.4 of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Posted the utilization report and status of implementation of PPAs under the BGCM in its website and at least three conspicuous places. Sent written notices to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate, and other offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly follow the guidelines in the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund as provided under Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121.
observations.managementAction: Strictly followed the utilization guideline as provided under Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121.

Posted reports on the LGU-Website. Prepared inventory list and coordinated with concerned offices. Submitted the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to COA. Adhered to COA Circulars.
observations.reason: Subject to continuous monitoring.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management immediately distribute and consume the in-kind donations and be cautious of its perishable nature to safeguard from wastage and loss.
observations.managementAction: Distributed in-kind donations and consumed immediately.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the BAC to submit the supporting documentary requirements and to observe strict adherence to the provisions of the above-mentioned circular.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required supporting documents and observed the provisions of the mentioned circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to update themselves regularly with the latest issuances of the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) particularly on the reporting and posting Annual Procurement which includes requirements related to government procurement and COVID-19 procurement items.
observations.managementAction: Posted in the website and GPPB online portal the procurement data of all awarded contracts for Emergency Procurements and Supplemental Procurement requirements on November 4, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report together with the required supporting documents and submit the same to COA pursuant to COA Circular 2014-001 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

a. Instruct the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the Municipality; and

b. Include all PPAs implemented and to be implemented in the status report to be submitted to COA for proper monitoring.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of projects. Included all PPAs in the status report submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Engineering Office to closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the projects, for the concerned officers to exercise due diligence in the conduct of the inspection and to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the preparation of the ABC set forth in DPWH Department Order No. 197 Series of 2016.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Engineering Office to closely monitor and supervise the implementation of the projects and to exercise due diligence in the conduct of the inspection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exercise diligence in conducting the bidding process and followed the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, submit to COA the clarification on the reason for using SVP since the contract price exceeded the threshold, and the lacking documents as enumerated above, to enable the Audit Team to review the contract in its entirety.
observations.managementAction: Exercised due diligence in conducting the bidding process and followed the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Clarification was not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that Management exert maximum effort to collect the
long outstanding receivables due from identified beneficiaries and
employees through issuance of demand letters and continually follow-up
to enforce collection.

2.7 We recommended that the Management undertake the following
measures:
a. Prepare and maintain stock and property cards for every type of
inventories and PPEs for proper monitoring;
b. Perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to ensure
that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and
reconciled with the books of accounts;
c. Instruct the Inventory Committee to exert more effort to complete
the conduct of physical inventory of all Inventory and PPE accounts
and submit the Report not later than January 31 each year pursuant
to Government Accounting Manual for LGUs;
d. Establish the accountability for all types of PPE to heighten the
security that the assets are properly safeguarded against wastage
and loss;
e. Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated
reconciling items supported with sufficient and appropriate
documents in order to reflect the correct account balances; and
f. Instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its effort
to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the unidentified
balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in order to render a
reliable and accurate financial report.

3.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Ensure that the monthly utilization report and status of
implementation of PPAs under the BGCM fund is posted in its
website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the
locality to promote transparency on the receipt and disbursements
of funds and public accountability thereof; and
b. Send written notice that the said reports were posted on its website
to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines,
House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on
Finance, and other offices where the submission of reports is
required in compliance with Item 4.4 of DBM LBC No. 125.

4.6 We recommended that the Management strictly follow the guidelines in
the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund as provided under Section 21 of Republic Act No. 10121.

5.5 We recommended that the Management:
a. Post the reports on the receipt and utilization of DRRMF sourced
from GAA and donations in the LGU-Website;
b. Instruct the GSO Officer to coordinate with other offices in
regards to the donated items for the maintenance of an inventory
list thereof;
c. Prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and
Balances together with the required supporting documents and
submit the same promptly to COA; and
d. Adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular
No. 2020-009 on the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
receipt and utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Fund, cash and in-kind donations.

6.6 We recommended that the Management distribute and consume
immediately the in-kind donations and be cautious of its perishable
nature to safeguard from wastage and loss.

7.5 We recommended that the Management direct the BAC to submit the
supporting documentary requirements and observe adherence to the
provisions of the above-mentioned circular.

8.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC to
update themselves regularly with the latest issuances of the Government
Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) particularly on the reporting and
posting requirements related to government procurement and observe
strict compliance thereof to ensure transparency and accountability.

9.6 We recommended that the Management prepare the Annual GAD
Accomplishment Report together with the required supporting
documents cited in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC. No. 2013-01 and
submit the same to COA pursuant to COA Circular 2014-001.

10.6 We recommended that the Management:
a. Instruct the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of
on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could
avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and
economic condition of the people of the Municipality; and
b. Include all PPAs implemented and to be implemented in the status
report to be submitted to COA for proper monitoring.

We recommended that the Management confirm the authenticity of the receivable account with the concerned GOCC and in the books reconcile the affected accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entry/ies, if warranted, with supporting documentation.
observations.managementAction: Inquired with the concerned GOCC through written letter.
observations.reason: Response from the GOCC did not disclose whether the LGU has receivables in the books of the GOCC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: Conducted reconciliation of the accounts and made the necessary adjusting entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exert maximum effort to collect the long outstanding receivables due from identified beneficiaries and employees through issuance of demand letters and continually follow-up to enforce collection.
observations.managementAction: Enforced collection on some of receivables.
observations.reason: Enforcement of collection still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management undertake the following measures: a.Perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to ensure that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts; b.Instruct the Inventory Committee to exert more effort to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all Inventory and PPE accounts; c.Establish the accountability for all types of PPE to heighten the security that the assets are properly safeguarded against wastage and loss; d.Prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items supported with sufficient and appropriate documents in order to reflect the correct account balances; and e.Instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its effort to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the unidentified balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in order to render a reliable and accurate financial report.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the offices to continue reconciliation and prepared the necessary adjusting entry.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a.Instruct the Accountant to stop the practice of directly deducting the Due to BIR account and observe the proper accounting and recording of the transactions; and b.Establish the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to observe proper accounting and recording of the said transfer and to show the actual flow of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: Stopped the practice of directly deducting the Due to BIR account and observed the proper accounting and recording of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management: a.Submit a written justification detailing the reasons for terminating the first contract and terminating the procurement; and b.Strictly adhere to the guidelines for termination of procurement contract provided in Annex I of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to promote fairness in the termination of procurement contracts and to prescribe contract conditions and measures to enable government to protect its interests.
observations.managementAction: Familiarized the guidelines on termination of contracts to exercise due diligence in future transactions.
observations.reason: No written justification received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management install sound internal control system on the cash transactions and operations of the Municipality and ensure that duties are properly segregated to minimize the risk of unauthorized use and misapplication of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Designated another liquidating officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the pictures of signages as part of the supporting documents be required to the Municipal Accountant as part of the supporting documents for claims of mobilization fee as the case may be.
observations.managementAction: Required the pictures of the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Billboards/Signages did not have complete supporting information.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to exert effort in locating the Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations issued by the Donee-Agencies, otherwise request the Donees to provide their copy of the said document certified as true copy from the original. It is also recommended to observe the proper recording and accounting of relief assistance extended during calamity.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the Acknowledgement Receipt.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management inform the DILG concerned in writing whenever changes or revisions are made thereto and use the latest format of the accomplishment report prescribed in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC. No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Used the latest format of the accomplishment report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in evaluating expenditures that are eligible for funding out of the Confidential Fund pursuant to Item 3.23 of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of the Commission on Audit (COA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), Commission on Audit (COA), Department of National Defense (DND).
observations.managementAction: Exercised prudence in evaluating expenditures that are eligible for funding out of the Confidential Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission of all documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the procuring entity furnish a copy of the contracts to COA within five (5) days upon perfection to the COA Office as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and submit the lacking documents as enumerated to enable the Audit Team to review the contract in its entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end without action.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the contracts to COA.
observations.reason: Some contracts were still submitted beyond the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management instruct those officials involved in the procurement activities to follow the standard rate on the sale of bidding documents as prescribed in the GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012.
observations.managementAction: Followed the bidding documents set under GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a.Instruct the MENRO to develop more its implementation of Solid Waste Management in accordance with the provisions stated in RA 9003; b.Suggest that the MENRO implement and search for other waste cost-efficient diversion methods that could help minimize or mitigate the possible hazardous effects of the non-establishment of Sanitary Landfill; and c.Monitor the implementation of solid waste management in the Barangay levels and provide assistance for the barangays to properly comply provisions of the RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Looked for possible alternatives which could help minimize or mitigate the budgetary constraints.
observations.reason: Sanitary Landfill was not established due to budgetary constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended: a.Instruct the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the Municipality; b.Include all PPAs implemented and to be implemented in the status report to be submitted to COA for proper monitoring; and c.Stop the practice of using the 20% Development Fund to pay for the prizes of the winning Barangays and prioritize the developmental projects that requires immediate consideration.
observations.managementAction: Fast tracked the implementation of projects.
observations.reason: There are still PPAs charged to 20% DF that were not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management intensify its efforts to generate additional income from the operations of the Catering Services to demonstrate its financial viability in sustaining its operations over the long term while delivering quality goods and services to the general public, and to show that it can be a source of additional funds to the General Fund to support the programs and activities of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Intensified its efforts to generate additional income from the operations of the Catering Services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a.Require those concerned officials to submit explanation/justification regarding the deficiencies noted for evaluation of our COA-TAS; b.Direct those concerned officials to meticulously prepare and review the POWs to ensure that only the required number of materials are included in the plan to avoid unnecessary costs, and to conduct thorough inspection of the projects to determine that this is in accordance with the approved Program of Works; c.Require those concerned officials in the preparation of the POWs and inspection of the projects to refund the deficiencies noted; and d.Instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor the refund made by the persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Directed the concerned officials to meticulously prepare and review the POWs.
observations.reason: There are still deficiencies noted during the conduct of Technical Evaluation and Inspection of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and those concerned officials in the preparation, review and approval of the program of works monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings by the COA TAS and render satisfactory explanation on the deficiencies noted in the COA technical review of the project. 

Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to deduct from the amount due to the concerned contractor the cost deficiencies or to demand payment for such amount, as the case may be.
observations.reason: The LGU terminated the contract, however, proper contract termination was not observed by the Management, thus we issued Finding No. 6.

observations.recommendation: 1.6	We recommended that the Management confirm the authenticity of the receivable account with the concerned GOCC and reconcile the affected accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entry/ies, if warranted, with supporting documentation.

2.6	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments, if any.

3.5   We recommended that the Management exert maximum effort to collect the long outstanding receivables due from identified beneficiaries and employees through issuance of demand letters and continually follow-up to enforce collection.

5.5		When transferring funds, establish a reciprocal account which is Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to set as a control in monitoring the transferred amount and only upon fulfilling the purpose for which the fund was transferred and closed.
5.6	We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of directly deducting the Due to BIR account when transferring the withheld taxes from funds other than the General Fund Proper; and
Establish the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to observe proper accounting and recording of the said transfer and to show the actual flow of the transactions.

6.8	We recommended that the Management:
Submit a written justification detailing the reasons for terminating the first contract and for non-adjustment of the Approved Budget of the Contract (ABC); and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines for contract termination provided in Annex I of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to promote fairness in the termination of procurement contracts and to prescribe contract conditions and measures to enable government to protect its interests.

7.6	We recommended that the Management install sound internal control system on the cash transactions and operations of the Municipality and ensure that duties are properly segregated to minimize the risk of unauthorized use and misapplication of government funds.

8.6	We recommended that the Management adhere to COA Circular No. 2013-004 relative to the installation of Billboards/Signages for the information and publicity of Programs/Projects/Activities implemented by the Municipality to promote transparency and involvement of the public in the transaction entered by the government.
8.7	We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant will require as part of the supporting documents the pictures of the signage being placed in the construction site for claims of mobilization fee as the case may be.

9.7	We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to exert effort in locating the Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations for DRRM issued by the Donee-Agencies, otherwise request the Donees to provide their copy of the said document certified as true copy from the original. It is also recommended to observe the proper recording and accounting of relief assistance extended during calamity.

10.5	We recommended that the Management inform the DILG concerned in writing whenever changes or revisions are made in the GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA to ensure that the revised activities still met the identified gender issues and concerns prevalent to the Municipality. Moreover, it is recommended that the LGU use the latest format of the accomplishment report prescribed in Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC. No. 2013-01.

11.5	We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in evaluating expenditures that are eligible for funding out of the Confidential Fund pursuant to Item 3.23 of Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 of the Commission on Audit (COA), Department of Budget and Management (DBM), Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG), Governance Commission for GOCCs and Department of National Defense (DND).

12.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Budget Officer to follow the provisions stated in Joint Circular No. 2015-001 dated January 8, 2015, to ensure the submission of all documentary requirements needed in the granting of cash advances for Confidential Fund.

13.10We recommended that the procuring entity furnish a copy of the contract within five (5) days upon perfection to the COA Office as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
13.11Likewise, please submit to COA the lacking documents as enumerated above, to enable us to review the contract in its entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end without action.
13.12Further, please be informed that auditorial contract review is required in the technical review and inspection by the Technical Information and Technology Services Office, COA Regional Office No. XIII, Butuan City.

14.6	We recommended that the Management instruct those officials involve in the procurement activities to follow the standard rate on the sale of bidding documents as prescribed in the GPPB Resolution No. 04-2012.

15.11We recommended that the Management review the rules and regulations on Alternative Methods of Procurement. Likewise, please submit to COA the clarification on the reason for using SVP since the contract price exceeded the threshold, and the lacking documents as enumerated above, to enable the Audit Team to review the contract in its entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end without action.
15.12Moreover, please be informed that auditorial contract review is required before forwarding the same for technical review and inspection by the COA Technical Information and Technology Services Office. Inspection of the procured items will be delayed if contract review will not be undertaken in advance by the COA-TAS, thus immediate submission of the lacking documents is required.
15.11Further, we recommended that the Management exercise diligence in conducting the bidding process and to follow the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

16.9 We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the MENRO to develop more its implementation of Solid Waste Management in accordance with the provisions stated in RA 9003 otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000;
Suggest that the MENRO implement and search for other waste cost-efficient diversion methods that could help minimize or mitigate the possible hazardous effects of the non-establishment of Sanitary Landfill; and
Monitor the implementation of solid waste management in the Barangay levels and provide assistance for the barangays to properly comply provisions of the RA 9003.

17.10 We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Implementing Office to fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects so that the constituents could avail of the benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the Municipality;
Include all PPAs implemented and to be implemented in the status report to be submitted to COA for proper monitoring; and
Stop the practice of using the 20% Development Fund to pay for the prizes of the winning Barangays and prioritize the developmental projects that requires immediate consideration.

18.7	We recommended that the Management intensify its efforts to generate additional income and cash from the operations of the Catering Services to demonstrate its financial viability in sustaining its operations over the long term while delivering quality goods and services to the general public, and to show that it can be a source of additional funds to the General Fund to support the programs and activities of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:

a. Exert extra effort in finding ways to liquidate long outstanding cash advance and issue demand letters to all accountable officers to settle all unliquidated cash advances.

b. To institute appropriate controls for employees to settle their accountabilities before their separation from the service from the Municipality.

c. To strictly adhere to Section 89 of PD 1445, Section 5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Item 6.0 and 9.0 of COA Circular 2012-004.
observations.managementAction: Full liquidation of cash advances on or before yearend was implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality undertake the following measures:

1) perform periodic reconciliation of stock/property cards to ensure that discrepancies are immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled with the books of accounts. 

2) instruct the Inventory Committee to exert more effort to complete the conduct of physical inventory of all PPE accounts and to transfer the physical count of the inventories to RPCI instead of RPCPPE;
3) prepare necessary journal entries to adjust the duly investigated reconciling items in order to reflect the correct account balances; and

4) instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to continue its effort to locate pertinent source documents to reduce the unidentified balances in the property ledger cards, if any, in order to render a complete and accurate financial report.
observations.reason:
On-going reconciliation of records between two offices.

Reiterated in Finding No. 4

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management develop more the dumping site to enhance the implementation of Solid Waste Management and to adhere to RA 9003 or otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the procuring entity submit a copy of the contract within five (5) days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

Likewise, please submit to COA the lacking documents as enumerated above, to enable us to review the contract in its entirety. All documents shall be properly authenticated or clear certified as true copies from the original otherwise, the same shall be returned to that end without action.

Otherwise, dissolution of the Motorpool as economic enterprise will be recommended if it still continue to incur losses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that   Management:                      Instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the payroll against the GSIS billing statement if monthly amortization for various GSIS loans availed by employees were deducted from their monthly compensation;                      Instruct also the Agency Authorized Officer for GSIS to diligently review employees’ loan applications before its approval, and see to it that net take home pay of the member is sufficient to cover the regular monthly amortization of the loan applied for; and                      Strictly deduct monthly GSIS loan amortization against the employees’ compensation pursuant to Section 14 of RIRR of RA No. 8291, to avoid surcharges and penalties to be imposed by the GSIS.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD fund for the year so that wider scope of intended beneficiaries can be benefited of the GAD programs, projects and activities, and objective of the fund can be fully achieved during the year.

Otherwise, dissolution of the Motorpool as economic enterprise will be recommended if it still continue to incur losses.

We recommend that Management submit the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report within 5 days from end of January of the preceding year together with the required supporting documents.

We recommend that Management stop the practice of procuring goods and services through cash advance and comply strictly to Sec. 93 of PD 1445 and the guidelines and principles of an imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier for internal control purposes.
It is also recommended to instruct the Bid and Awards Committee to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 52 and 53 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the conditions before the LGU can adopt shopping and negotiated procurement as alternative modes of procurement.

We recommend that Management require the concerned personnel to conduct periodic physical inventory count of supplies and submit Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
Further, we recommend that the General Services Officer maintain supplies stock cards for each type of supplies and reconcile these with the ledger cards maintained in the Accounting Office.

6.5	We recommend that Management require the responsible officials to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009 by informing COA Auditor through written request or submission of delivery receipts of inventories, specifically consumable items on or within 24 hours after delivery of procured inventories or materials for proper checking as to the correctness/accuracy and existence of deliveries.
We recommend that Management exert maximum effort to collect the long outstanding receivables through sending demand letters and statement of account and follow up to enforce collection.

8.9.	We recommend that Inventory Committee should conduct actual physical inventory of all its PPE annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures.
Planning
The GSO chief prepares the inventory guidelines and sends it to Agency head for approval.
Definite plans should be developed concerning the date of physical inventory, who is to supervise the count, the personnel to assist and the sequence of counting.
Set of written instructions should be prepared to be observed by each person connected with the taking of inventory.
AREs should be ready for efficiency and fast work.
Tags should be serially pre-numbered and prepared in duplicate.
Execution
All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated and cleared immediately. If necessary, written explanations shall be required from persons responsible.
Reconciliation of GSO and Accounting Records
After the physical inventory-taking, the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records.
The inventory listing of the equipment shall be checked against the ledger card maintained by the Property and the Ledger cards maintained by the accounting and finally against the control account.
Exclude items with serviceable life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2005-002 dated April 14, 2005.
PPEs with no assigned AREs found in station should be issued with AREs.

9.6	We recommend that Management require the Municipality's BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

10.7	We recommend that Management send demand letters to barangays for the immediate liquidation of the fund transferred to determine if the purpose for which it has been transferred has been served.
10.8	It is also recommended to verify and monitor the implementation of projects funded out of the transferred funds especially for long outstanding downloaded funds and demand return of the fund if found not utilized or used.

11.8	We recommend that the management instruct the Municipal Local School Board to:
Maximize the utilization of the Special Education Fund budget in a year in order to attain the objectives for which the fund was intended;
Prioritize the funding of programs, projects and activities stipulated in Section 272 of the Local Government Code and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1 s. 1998 dated April 14, 1998, 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and 01-B dated June 25, 2001 in the Annual and Supplemental Budget for SEF; and
Require the Municipal Local School Board to submit the School Improvement Plan (SIP) for CY 2016 as basis of charging expenditures to SEF.

12.7	We recommend that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly observe the afore-cited regulation to furnish the Office of the Auditor of certified copies of the suspension orders for its immediate determination if the grounds for suspension is reasonable or not.
12.8	Further, the MEO should ensure that projects will be completed within the construction period as stipulated in the contract.

13.6	We recommend that MEO fast track the implementation of on-going and unimplemented projects and ensure that projects will be completed within the target date of completion so that the constituents could avail of the timely benefits of the programs and improve the living and economic condition of the people of the municipality.

14.8	We recommend that the management resort into alternative mode of procurement for project and programs that was not implemented due to failure of bidding and maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF allocation for disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation so that greater number of its constituents can be benefited.

1.5	We recommend that Management:

a) 	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare statement/schedule of overdue Due from LGUs to inform the Management of status of fund transfers as basis of the latter to issue letter to concerned barangay officials for immediate submission of Accomplishment and Fund Utilization Reports as agreed upon in the MOA. 

b) 	Demand from the concerned barangay officials the refund of the full amount granted in the event that the requesting implementing agency fails to undertake the project.

c) 	Stop the granting of fund transfers to barangay who failed to submit accomplishment and liquidation reports.
observations.managementAction: Monthly the Municipal Accountant furnish copy of the Trial balance with corresponding schedules of accounts and that includes the Due from LGU’s for the information of the LCE. Only two barangay left unliquidated, Brgy. Anahawan and Brgy. Kioya

observations.recommendation:
2.4	We recommend that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to identify and trace the causes of said unreconciled balances of the intra-agency accounts and effects its reconciliation; and

Maintain and regularly update subsidiary ledgers for affected accounts to support the general ledger balances; facilitate the early detection of errors, expedite adjustments, and determine if total of the individual accounts tally with the general ledger.
observations.managementAction: Unreconciled balances already adjusted as of February 2016 accounting reports

observations.recommendation: 3.5 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office the complete list of taxpayers who have tax due as of the year in order to set the correct and actual amount of receivables and liability accounts. Also, require the Accountant to refrain from effecting such entry in the books which caused the incurrence of negative balance of the subject accounts.
observations.managementAction: List of taxpayers (Basic & SEF) have been submitted to the accounting office

observations.recommendation:
4.5 We therefore recommend that Management:
a. 	Direct the Supply Officer to secure copy of the DR for all supplies received from the PS-DBM and attached the same to its respective DV. The IAR should include only those items actually received and for the concerned supply officer to make a notation for partial acceptance and to take note of the undelivered items and make a constant follow up with the PS-DBM to ensure full delivery of the balance, if there are any.  

b. 	Require the Accountant to record the undelivered supplies under the Due from Other NGAs account and Office Supplies Inventory/Expenses for the delivered items to reflect an accurate and reliable balance of accounts. The subsidiary ledgers should also be maintained for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction:
GSO:
Secured delivery receipt in every supplies receive from PS-DBM

Accounting:
Recorded due from other NGAS account for undelivered supplies of the PS DBM upon receipt of supporting documents from the GSO

observations.recommendation:
5.7 We recommend that Management shall:

(1) 	Direct those concerned officers to complete the inventory taking and submit the report to the Accounting Office for reconciliation. Any discrepancy shall be investigated and traced to the source documents and effect the necessary adjustments to the respective records of the concerned offices; and

(2) 	Require the Accountant to make an adjusting entry to recognize the fuel already consumed as expense, provided that supporting documents shall be completely attached to the voucher prior to its payment.
observations.managementAction: Started preparing inventory reports

observations.recommendation:
6.5 We recommend to the Management that:

A task force, composing of personnel from Accounting and GSO, be created to conduct the reconciliation of the affected accounts as soon as the physical count is complete; and

The Accounting Office should prepare Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust reconciling items identified with sufficient and appropriate supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Physical count is currently on going, that is physical reconciliation will be done after complete physical inventory report is submitted in the accounting office

observations.recommendation: 7.6 Direct the Supply Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and the rules and procedures provided in COA Circular 92-386, in order to prevent further deterioration and generate additional income for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Unserviceable properties already disposed

observations.recommendation: 8.5	Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to demand from the supplier for the reissuance of warranty certificate that indicates a minimum of 1 year warranty for the above-cited units. Henceforth, the BAC should emphasize to the bidders during bidding that the warranty certificate to be issued by the winning bidder for a specific procurement should be in accordance with Section 62 of the RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Secured warranty certificate that indicates a minimum of 1 year warranty.

observations.recommendation: 9.10 We recommend that Management (a) stop the practice of procuring goods and services thru cash advance; (b) comply strictly with the guidelines and principles of an imprest fund wherein regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should all be paid in check in the name of the supplier for internal control purposes and so that the applicable taxes may be deducted from the purchases; and (c) adhere to the provisions of Sections 52 and 53 of  the Revised IRR of RA 9184 on the conditions before the LGU can adopt shopping and negotiated procurement as alternative modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Minimized the payment if purchase of goods and services through cash advance

observations.recommendation:
10.6 In view of the above circumstance, we recommend the following to the Management:
(a) 	Require the Municipal Accountant to book-up transfer of funds to establish the correct amount of receivable and payable accounts in the books of General Fund-Proper and LEE-Catering;

(b) 	Adoption of cost-cutting measures to lessen expenses and boost income; and 

(c) 	Instruct in-charged to return fund transferred/advanced out of profits derived from operation of LEE after deducting the cost of improvement, repair and other related expenses of the LEE.
observations.managementAction: As recommended, the Php650,000.00 transfer from General fund was already booked-up in February 2016 reports.

observations.recommendation: 12.6 We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to require the concerned processors of the subject DVs to submit the lacking documentary requirements for evaluation in audit. Henceforth, any payment of claims should be supported with complete documentation to establish the validity and regularity of transactions, otherwise the same shall be subjected to suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Secured the supporting documents

observations.recommendation:
13.7 In view of the above circumstance, we recommend the following to the Management:

(a) 	Stop the practice of classifying the amount derived from issuance of Sworn Statement of True Value and other collections as trust liability, instead recognize the same as income; and 

(b) 	Refrain from utilizing such funds and transfer the remaining balance to unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: Already stop the practice of classifying as trust liabilities the receipts of fees from sworn statement and recorded already as income

observations.recommendation: 14.3 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the amount of Php8,725.11 during the payment of billing, otherwise, a Notice of Disallowance will be issued on the same amount Moreover, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Technical Working Group to meticulously review all the documents and the basis of the Approved Budget of the Contract before proceeding to the procurement process.
observations.managementAction:
Every contract review findings are diligently applied/deducted in the billing, whether in progress billing or final billing whenever claim is on process upon receipt of the contract review result.

In this case, the amount of P8,725.11 was already deducted on the first billing (90% accomplishment) per voucher no. 101-2016-01-41 dated 1/20/16

observations.recommendation: 15.6 We therefore recommend to the LCE to direct the Municipal Engineer to submit the computation on the extent of delay taking into account the approved variation orders and the duly approved time extension for evaluation in audit. Henceforth, observe strict compliance of the provisions of RIRR of R.A. 9184 relative to the grant of time extension to provide reasonable basis in the incurrence of delayed completion of the project to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The project is already completed

observations.recommendation: 16.4 We recommend to the Local Chief Executive to cause the formulation of an Annual Gender and Development (GAD) Plan and prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as provided for under Joint Circular No. 2012-01 issued by PCW, NEDA and DBM, so that there would be a proper implementation, monitoring and performance evaluation of GAD related projects/programs and activities.
observations.managementAction: Formulated GAD plan and prepared GAD accomplishment report
